events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 13 April: US-Iran talks falter; Strait of Hormuz uncertainty deepens
events.description: Weekend developments have raised fresh concerns worldwide after talks between the United States and Iran on Saturday ended without agreement, fuelling uncertainty and raising tensions once again. The planned US blockade targeting Iranian ports going into effect has added to fears of further escalation and global trade disruption. Meanwhile, the humanitarian toll mounts, with Lebanese authorities reporting that deaths from Israeli airstrikes has now surpassed 2,000. Stay with us for live updates on humanitarian impacts and diplomatic efforts. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 13 April: US-Iran talks falter; Strait of Hormuz uncertainty deepens

events.title: Development finance gap risks reversing decades of progress
events.description: Global fragmentation, deepening geopolitical tensions and conflicts are putting decades of development progress at risk, the UN warned in a report published on Thursday - calling for stepping up investment to meet internationally agreed goals.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Development finance gap risks reversing decades of progress

events.title: Sudan: Top UN aid official warns of ‘abandoned crisis’ as war enters fourth year
events.description: As Sudan’s war moves into a fourth year, civilians are still being killed, displaced and subjected to widespread sexual violence, the UN’s top humanitarian official in the country warned on Monday, calling for urgent action to stop the fighting and protect civilians.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan: Top UN aid official warns of ‘abandoned crisis’ as war enters fourth year

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 9 April: UN condemns massive strikes in Lebanon, as concerns grow over ceasefire
events.description: With a US-Iran ceasefire offering a fragile glimmer of hope after weeks of conflict, violence continues to reverberate across the Middle East. Massive airstrikes in Lebanon have caused heavy civilian casualties and widespread destruction, drawing strong UN condemnation. As humanitarian needs deepen and diplomatic efforts intensify, the situation remains highly volatile. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 9 April: UN condemns massive strikes in Lebanon, as concerns grow over ceasefire

events.title: Lebanon airstrike casualties ‘still under the rubble’ as ambulances, hospitals face new threats
events.description: With Lebanon still reeling from Israel’s devastating airstrikes on 8 April, UN humanitarians reported new fears of attacks on ambulances and looming food shortages in the south of the country on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon airstrike casualties ‘still under the rubble’ as ambulances, hospitals face new threats

events.title: France: Ex-Lafarge CEO jailed for funding Syrian jihadists
events.description: The world's largest cement maker paid off terrorist groups in Syria in order to keep manufacturing there, judges in Paris have ruled.
events.ticker_label: 💥 France: Ex-Lafarge CEO jailed for funding Syrian jihadists

events.title: Lebanon will need '1 billion dollars just to keep humanitarian situation afloat', minister says
events.description: FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with the Lebanese Minister for Social Affairs, Hanin Sayyed, about the humanitarian crisis in Lebanon in the wake of the Israeli attacks and ground invasion. She says that if the war continues and displacement in Lebanon continues at the current pace, the country will need "1 billion dollars in a 12-month period to be able to just keep the humanitarian situation afloat".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon will need '1 billion dollars just to keep humanitarian situation afloat', minister says

events.title: Russian strikes on Odesa kill 2 ahead of Orthodox Easter ceasefire
events.description: Russian drone strikes killed at least two people and wounded two others in the Ukrainian city of Odesa overnight Saturday, hitting a residential area
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russian strikes on Odesa kill 2 ahead of Orthodox Easter ceasefire

events.title: Weather tracker: Super Typhoon Sinlaku threatens Mariana Islands
events.description: Rapidly strengthening storm brings destructive winds, flooding risk and dangerous seas to western Pacific Dangerous super typhoon barreling toward group of remote US islands The Mariana Islands archipelago in the western Pacific, home to the US territories of Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands, are bracing for extreme weather early this week as Super Typhoon Sinlaku approaches. The system originated as a cluster of thunderstorms over the seas of Micronesia before strengthening into a tropical storm and then a typhoon on Friday and Saturday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: Super Typhoon Sinlaku threatens Mariana Islands

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 10 April: Fear and uncertainty grow in Lebanon as conflict grinds on
events.description: Fighting continues across parts of the Middle East, with renewed exchanges between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon. Humanitarian needs are deepening, with rising civilian casualties, mounting displacement and growing strain on services. Diplomatic efforts continue with high stakes negotiations due to start Saturday between the US and Iran over the faltering ceasefire deal. Lebanon is an active war zone and the Strait of Hormuz is still effectively closed. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 10 April: Fear and uncertainty grow in Lebanon as conflict grinds on

events.title: Deadline passes for US blockade of Hormuz, Iran threatens to retaliate against Gulf neighbours
events.description: The deadline passed on Monday for the start of a US ​military blockade of ships leaving Iran's ports, and Tehran threatened to retaliate against ports of its Gulf neighbours after weekend talks on ending the war broke down. Oil prices surged, with no sign of a swift reopening of the Strait of Hormuz to ease the biggest ever disruption in supplies. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Deadline passes for US blockade of Hormuz, Iran threatens to retaliate against Gulf neighbours

events.title: EU doubles steel tariffs to 50% to curb surge of cheap Chinese imports
events.description: EU lawmakers and member states agreed on Monday to double tariffs on foreign steel to 50%, aiming to shield the bloc’s struggling industry from a surge in cheap Chinese imports. The deal also cuts duty-free import volumes by 47%. Trade chief Maros Sefcovic warned global overcapacity threatens Europe’s industrial strength.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 EU doubles steel tariffs to 50% to curb surge of cheap Chinese imports

events.title: Lebanon: Health system overwhelmed following a ‘horrific’ day of Israeli strikes
events.description: The scale and speed of destruction from the wave of airstrikes in Lebanon which began just hours after the US-Iran ceasefire announcement, has left the country’s already strained health system struggling to cope, according to the World Health Organization (WHO).
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon: Health system overwhelmed following a ‘horrific’ day of Israeli strikes

events.title: New Zealand’s North Island braces for Cyclone Vaianu with thousands ordered to evacuate
events.description: Vaianu, forecast to bring heavy rain and winds of up to 130 kmh (80 mph), is expected to hit on Sunday Thousands of New Zealanders were ordered to evacuate their homes on Saturday as the country’s North Island braced for Cyclone Vaianu, which authorities warned could cause coastal flooding and landslides. Vaianu, forecast to bring heavy rain and winds of up to 130 km/h (80 mp/h), was expected to hit on Sunday, then pass west of the remote Chatham Islands on Monday, the country’s weather forecaster said. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ New Zealand’s North Island braces for Cyclone Vaianu with thousands ordered to evacuate

events.title: Iran war's global energy crisis sharpens China’s advantage in clean tech
events.description: The war in Iran is sharpening China’s advantage in promoting a renewable energy future, as oil disruptions expose the fragility of fossil fuel systems
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war's global energy crisis sharpens China’s advantage in clean tech

events.title: Antisemitic attacks in 2025 led to highest number of deaths in decades, study finds
events.description: A new study says that 2025 saw the highest number of deaths from antisemitic attacks in over 30 years around the world

events.title: Haiti’s freefall demands urgent global action as millions face hunger and violence
events.description: Haiti is facing “one of the most severe and rapidly deteriorating humanitarian crises in the Western Hemisphere,” a senior UN aid official warned on Friday, underscoring the need for continued global attention to alleviate suffering there.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Haiti’s freefall demands urgent global action as millions face hunger and violence

events.title: World News in Brief: Sudan refugee update, child trafficking crisis in South Sudan, ‘mystery’ illness in Burundi, human rights in Serbia
events.description: More than a million Sudanese refugees who’ve fled into neighbouring Chad risk losing out on essential food, shelter and other support due to funding cuts, as the war in their homeland approaches the three-year mark.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Sudan refugee update, child trafficking crisis in South Sudan, ‘mystery’ illness in Burundi, human rights in Serbia

events.title: Eight to be awarded for bravery in Bondi Junction stabbing
events.description: Six people were killed and ten injured after a man went on a stabbing rampage in an Australian mall.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Eight to be awarded for bravery in Bondi Junction stabbing

events.title: War in Iran could accelerate global energy transition
events.description: The Iran war has exposed vulnerabilities of countries reliant on Middle Eastern fossil fuels. Ramon Mendez, former Uruguayan Energy Secretary and President of REN21, tells France 24 that a drastic shift to renewable energy is possible with the right strategy in place, and other nations can follow Uruguay's footsteps - the South American country now gets 98% of its electricity grid from low-carbon sources.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War in Iran could accelerate global energy transition

events.title: Hungary's Orban defeated: France's far-right loses an ally
events.description: Congratulations from across Europe poured in Monday for Hungarian political newcomer Peter Magyar, who defeated nationalist Viktor Orban in elections seen as a blow to right-wing populism worldwide. In France, the outcome represents a setback for Marine Le Pen’s far-right party, as FRANCE 24’s French Politics Editor Marc Perelman explains.

events.title: US-Iran war: Guterres calls for continued talks, end to ceasefire violations
events.description: The United States and Iran must continue talks aimed at ending their weeks-long war, UN Secretary-General António Guterres said on Monday, underscoring that there is no military solution to the conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Iran war: Guterres calls for continued talks, end to ceasefire violations

events.title: From conflict to catwalk: Women ex-combatants weave reconciliation in Colombia
events.description: Katerine Avella is a former combatant in Colombia’s decades-long civil war, a peace signatory and a community leader. After the guns fell silent, she created the fashion brand Ixora but, with violence returning to the region, Ms. Avella is now focusing on trying to keep the project afloat in the face of new challenges.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 From conflict to catwalk: Women ex-combatants weave reconciliation in Colombia

events.title: Putting humans at the centre: UN AI panel begins work on global impact study
events.description: The UN’s Independent International Scientific Panel on AI – the first global body of its kind – is gearing up for its inaugural in-person summit.

events.title: Sudan: 14 million displaced; hunger and attacks on health continue as war enters fourth year
events.description: As Sudan approaches the third anniversary of a brutal civil war, millions remain displaced and hungry while the health system lies in ruins, with no end to the violence in sight, UN agencies said on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan: 14 million displaced; hunger and attacks on health continue as war enters fourth year

events.title: Manhunt underway after gunmen storm Chick-fil-A leaving 1 dead
events.description: Authorities say a deadly shooting at a New Jersey Chick-fil-A was targeted as suspects remain at large. One person died and six others were injured.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Manhunt underway after gunmen storm Chick-fil-A leaving 1 dead

events.title: North Korean leader supervises missile tests from his naval destroyer
events.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un supervised more missile tests from his prized new warship over the weekend, state media said Tuesday, as he pledged to boost the navy’s operational capabilities with his growing collection of nuclear-capable weapons
events.ticker_label: 🪖 North Korean leader supervises missile tests from his naval destroyer

events.title: Illegal immigrant accused of Easter kidnapping, sex assault in college town; DHS rips second attack in weeks
events.description: Federal authorities warn Missouri not to release an illegal immigrant accused of raping and kidnapping a woman in Kirksville on Easter Sunday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Illegal immigrant accused of Easter kidnapping, sex assault in college town; DHS rips second attack in weeks

events.title: IDF uncovers Hezbollah weapons stash inside hospital in Lebanon
events.description: Israel Defense Forces uncovered a weapons stash used by Hezbollah terrorists inside a hospital in Lebanon this weekend.
events.ticker_label: 💥 IDF uncovers Hezbollah weapons stash inside hospital in Lebanon

events.title: Floods, power outages and hundreds evacuated as Cyclone Vaianu lashes New Zealand’s North Island
events.description: Cyclone crossed coast near Maketu peninsula, packing destructive winds exceeding 130km/h (80 mph), heavy rain and large swells Cyclone Vaianu made landfall in New Zealand’s North Island on Sunday, triggering floods, power outages and forcing hundreds to evacuate . The cyclone crossed the coast near the Maketu peninsula, packing destructive winds exceeding 130km/h (80 mph), heavy rain and large swells, national weather provider MetService said, describing Vaianu as a “life-threatening” system. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Floods, power outages and hundreds evacuated as Cyclone Vaianu lashes New Zealand’s North Island

events.title: Pope Leo: I am not afraid of Trump administration
events.description: The pontiff stressed that his increasingly strong anti-war statements are not attacks on any one person.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pope Leo: I am not afraid of Trump administration

events.title: ‘Clock is ticking’: Hormuz disruption raises fears of global food crisis
events.description: The clock is ticking for global food systems as disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz threaten to choke off the flow of fuel and crucial fertilizers needed for the next planting season – also raising the risk of higher food prices and a new wave of inflation.

events.title: ‘Gate of Tears’ at risk: Iran threatens major new global chokepoint if US moves on Hormuz
events.description: Senior Middle East advisor warns Iran could use Houthi proxies to blockade the Bab al-Mandeb strait if the U.S. blocks the Strait of Hormuz.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Gate of Tears’ at risk: Iran threatens major new global chokepoint if US moves on Hormuz

events.title: Ghanaian winger Dominic Frimpong killed at age of 20 in attack on team bus
events.description: Armed men fired at Berekum Chelsea bus on Sunday Frimpong dies of wounds at hospital Berekum Chelsea winger Dominic Frimpong was killed in an armed robbery on his team’s bus as they returned from a match on Sunday, the Ghana Football Association said. Berekum Chelsea said six “masked men wielding guns and assault rifles” had blocked the road as the team returned from their Ghana Premier League match against Samartex. Continue reading...

events.title: Colombia to cull up to 80 hippos descended from Pablo Escobar zoo
events.description: Dozens of feral pachyderms linked to drug kingpin to be killed because of threat to native species and villagers Colombian officials have authorized a plan to cull dozens of hippos descended from animals brought to the country in the 1980s by Pablo Escobar, after the feral beasts displaced native species and threatened local villagers. The environment minister, Irene Vélez, said the decision was reached because other methods to control their population had been expensive and unsuccessful, including neutering some of the animals or moving them to zoos. Vélez said that up to 80 hippos would be affected by the measure. She did not say when the hunting would begin. Continue reading...

events.title: Anthony Albanese urges Israel to stop Lebanon attacks that intensified during Middle East ceasefire
events.description: PM tells Guardian Australia Hezbollah should cease reprisals and confirms Australia’s military surveillance aircraft will remain in region Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s prime minister, Anthony Albanese, has urged Israel to stop its attacks on Lebanon and raised concern over its intensified military campaign on Beirut and the country’s south after the ceasefire in the Middle East. Albanese also called on Hezbollah to cease attacks on Israel, reiterating his government’s belief that the Middle East ceasefire must include Lebanon . The prime minister also confirmed Australia’s military surveillance aircraft would remain in the region for at least another month beyond its initial deployment. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Anthony Albanese urges Israel to stop Lebanon attacks that intensified during Middle East ceasefire

events.title: Trump vows US will 'eliminate' any Iranian ships defying blockade
events.description: US President Donald Trump has warned that any Iranian vessels that breach Monday's newly enforced US naval blockade will be “immediately eliminated,” escalating tensions amid a fragile ceasefire after talks brokered by Pakistan failed to reach a peace agreement on the conflict in Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump vows US will 'eliminate' any Iranian ships defying blockade

events.title: Donald Trump 'hasn't read the psychology of Iranians well at all', military expert says
events.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, former British Military Intelligence officer Philip Ingram says that "Donald Trump hasn't read the psychology of the Iranians well at all" adding that "the Iranians are not going to give in, and therefore Donald Trump either has to continue the blockade and deal with the impact that that's going to have on global economies [...] or go back to a bombing campaign".
events.ticker_label: 💥 Donald Trump 'hasn't read the psychology of Iranians well at all', military expert says

events.title: Inside Tehran after strikes: Iranian woman describes fear, checkpoints and people used as ‘human shields’
events.description: An anonymous Iranian woman detailed to The Australian the nightly explosions, celebrations and fear in Tehran as Trump's two-week ceasefire with Iran tentatively took hold.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Inside Tehran after strikes: Iranian woman describes fear, checkpoints and people used as ‘human shields’

events.title: Nigeria Air Force orders investigation after dozens killed in airstrike
events.description: There are reports that more than 100 people were killed, although the authorities have not commented.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Nigeria Air Force orders investigation after dozens killed in airstrike

events.title: Pro-EU Hungarian PM-elect Magyar vows to bring in 'new era'
events.description: From Madrid to Helsinki, many hope that Hungary's premier-elect Peter Magyar's win will help unshackle the 27-nation European Union as it faces hybrid warfare attacks from Moscow, an antagonistic Washington and Beijing's economic pressure. EU leaders had been increasingly frustrated with Orbán over his takeover of democratic institutions and vetoing of strategic action like a 90-billion-euro loan for Ukraine. It remains to be seen whether those hopes will be fulfilled, as FRANCE 24's Europe Editor Armen Georgian reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pro-EU Hungarian PM-elect Magyar vows to bring in 'new era'

events.title: Irish government announces measures to ease fuel crisis after days of protests
events.description: Ireland's prime minister Micheal Martin has announced measures to ease the pressures of fast-rising fuel prices following major protests across the country.

events.title: LA-area street takeover shooting caught on camera; locals scared, vow to move
events.description: Four people were hospitalized after gunfire erupted at a street takeover in Rosemead, authorities said, as LA County faces a wave of violent gatherings.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 LA-area street takeover shooting caught on camera; locals scared, vow to move

events.title: UN experts slam attacks on Gaza shelters, forced displacement in West Bank
events.description: Panel details incidents of violence by Israeli forces and settlers against displaced Palestinians in Gaza and West Bank.

events.title: Pope Leo brushes off Trump criticism amid growing Vatican–U.S. tensions over Iran war
events.description: Pope Leo XIV says he will not be deterred by criticism from President Trump, vowing to continue his calls for peace as tensions escalate between the Vatican and Washington over the Iran conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pope Leo brushes off Trump criticism amid growing Vatican–U.S. tensions over Iran war

events.title: US-Iran war: Is Pakistan a real peacemaker?
events.description: Islamabad has been praised for mediating the conflict between Iran and the US, but with an ongoing war with Kabul and a crackdown on the opposition at home, it needs to do some serious work to improve its stock.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Iran war: Is Pakistan a real peacemaker?

events.title: Haiti arrests 5 police officers and 2 other suspects over deadly fortress stampede
events.description: Authorities in Haiti have arrested seven suspects, including five police officers, after a fatal stampede killed at least 25 people at a historic mountaintop fortress over the weekend

events.title: Iran ceasefire raises hopes for reopening key Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The announcement of a shaky two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran will, it is hoped, lead to the opening of the strategically important Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway through which one fifth of the world’s oil and gas passes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran ceasefire raises hopes for reopening key Strait of Hormuz

events.title: 1-year-old injured after teen driver crashes car off Oklahoma bridge
events.description: Police say a vehicle went airborne off a Tulsa bridge and landed upside down on Interstate 244, injuring a 1-year-old child not in a car seat.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Two thirds of underage Australians still have access to social media despite ban - study
events.description: More than 60% of underage Australians are still using social media despite the country's world-first ban introduced four months ago, new research suggests.

events.title: Two arrested in possible shooting near OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home
events.description: Two people have been arrested after a gunshot was allegedly fired near OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's San Francisco home, according to a report.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Two arrested in possible shooting near OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home

events.title: Iran's supreme leader severely disfigured by US strikes: report
events.description: Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is severely disfigured after U.S.-Israel airstrikes on Tehran killed his father, according to a Reuters report citing anonymous sources.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's supreme leader severely disfigured by US strikes: report

events.title: UK finds attack on Taylor Swift-themed class 'preventable'
events.description: A new report on the stabbing that left little three girls dead in UK's Southport concluded that the killer's parents and state authorities failed to act on his violent behavior.

events.title: French court jails Lafarge former CEO for funding IS in Syria
events.description: A French court on Monday fined the cement group Lafarge over $1.3 million and sentenced its former boss to six years in prison for paying protection money to the Islamic State group and other jihadists to maintain its business in war-torn Syria. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 French court jails Lafarge former CEO for funding IS in Syria

events.title: US-Vatican feud over Iran: Pope Leo pushes back on Trump's criticism
events.description: The Pope pushed back Monday on President Donald Trump’s criticism of him over the US-Israel war in Iran, telling reporters that the Vatican’s appeals for peace and reconciliation are rooted in the Gospel, and that he doesn’t fear the Trump administration. Leo XIV's comments came as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz appeared to have come to a halt, an intelligence firm said, and oil prices resumed their climb after Trump announced on social media that the United States would blockade the waterway.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Vatican feud over Iran: Pope Leo pushes back on Trump's criticism

events.title: At least 30 killed in Haiti stampede
events.description: At least 30 people are dead after a stampede in Haiti.

events.title: UK report lays bare 'catastrophic' missed chances before stabbings at girls' dance class
events.description: An inquiry has found that a mass killing by a British teenager in 2024 could have been prevented if his parents and state agencies had acted on his violence fixation

events.title: Tech gaps and budget cuts threaten global efforts to track and help the vulnerable
events.description: Digital tools are changing the way countries manage everything from tracking refugees to caring for the elderly.

events.title: Iran carried out highest number of executions in decades
events.description: Organisations warn many more people could be executed in the wake of the anti-government protests which swept Iran earlier this year.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran carried out highest number of executions in decades

events.title: Israel kills three Palestinians in separate Gaza strikes
events.description: Two Palestinians were killed in separate strikes in southern Gaza on Monday, according to officials at Nasser hospital.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel kills three Palestinians in separate Gaza strikes

events.title: Hungarians look to changed future after pro-EU Magyar's election landslide
events.description: Hungarians were waking up to a ​political earthquake on Monday, after a landslide victory for the centre-right opposition reverberated everywhere from Washington to Kyiv, sending local markets surging and turning Budapest into a party zone. Magyar has pledged a sweeping anti-corruption drive, including stronger judicial independence and tougher public procurement rules to help secure the release of the EU funds.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Hungarians look to changed future after pro-EU Magyar's election landslide

events.title: Kosovo mission head warns ‘mistrust’ threatens post-election stability
events.description: Despite successful legislative elections in Kosovo late last year, a “delicate equilibrium” persists as deep divisions remain over the future of the United Nations presence in the region.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Kosovo mission head warns ‘mistrust’ threatens post-election stability

events.title: Hundreds feared dead in Lebanon strikes
events.description: The United Nations has strongly condemned airstrikes by the Israeli military across Lebanon on Wednesday which have resulted in significant casualties and destruction.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hundreds feared dead in Lebanon strikes

events.title: US military kills 2 suspected cartel operatives in latest Eastern Pacific lethal strike, SOUTHCOM says
events.description: The U.S. military killed two men believed to be cartel operatives in the Eastern Pacific as part of broader efforts to disrupt narcotics trafficking.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US military kills 2 suspected cartel operatives in latest Eastern Pacific lethal strike, SOUTHCOM says

events.title: Iran regime uses former Soviet republic to dodge sanctions, fund war machine: report
events.description: Once a committed U.S. ally, Georgia has quietly aligned with Iran, raising concerns about IRGC influence and sanctions evasion, a new report claims.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran regime uses former Soviet republic to dodge sanctions, fund war machine: report

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: ‘This scene is alive’: Abidjan art week showcases city as growing cultural hub
events.description: Late-night gallery tours and new venues signal a city staking its claim as a regional arts capital On a recent weekday evening, the doors of more than a dozen galleries and museums across Abidjan stayed open till midnight, several hours later than usual, as art enthusiasts went around town on a bus tour. It was the Night of the Galleries, designed for people to drop in after work and enjoy Abidjan art week to the fullest. The after-hours special showcase was first tested in January 2024 on the sidelines of the Africa Cup of Nations football tournament hosted and won by Côte d’Ivoire. The tradition continued this year during the art week’s third edition, which ran from last Tuesday to Sunday. Continue reading...

events.title: Cancelled safety upgrades on Sydney light rail would have only cost $2.2m, whistleblower claims after fatal incidents
events.description: NSW government urged to investigate if transport department refused to share cost of sensors that would detect a person entering coupling area Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The potentially life-saving safety upgrades to Sydney’s light rail, which a former Transdev employee has alleged were cancelled in order to save money, would have only cost about $2.2m, according to the whistleblower. The New South Wales government has been urged to investigate whether its transport department declined to share the cost of installing sensors that would detect a person entering the coupling area between two joined trams after a fatal incident in 2023. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump says U.S. will blockade Iranian ports. And, Orbán loses Hungarian election
events.description: President Trump announced a blockade of Iranian ports after peace talks with Iran collapsed. And, Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 16 years in power in Hungary.

events.title: Middle East war live: Israel-Lebanon talks set as Hezbollah urges Beirut to cancel negotiations
events.description: The leader of the Lebanese militant Hezbollah group urged Lebanon on Monday to pull out of direct talks with Israel set to take place in Washington the following day, the first such talks in decades. Naim Kassem spoke in a televised address on the eve of the scheduled meeting between Lebanon and Israel's ambassadors to the United States, as both sides set a framework for negotiations. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war live: Israel-Lebanon talks set as Hezbollah urges Beirut to cancel negotiations

events.title: Bodies of two iron workers recovered from rubble of Philadelphia parking garage 5 days after partial collapse
events.description: Philadelphia officials recovered the bodies of two iron workers from a collapsed parking garage. A third worker died at a hospital after being pulled from the rubble last week.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Bodies of two iron workers recovered from rubble of Philadelphia parking garage 5 days after partial collapse

events.title: Man climbs onto US military aircraft in Ireland, attacks it with hatchet: report
events.description: A man allegedly climbed onto a U.S. military aircraft at Ireland's Shannon Airport and attacked it with a hatchet, according to local police reports.

events.title: Trump says he won't apologize to Pope Leo after criticism
events.description: President Donald Trump refused to apologize to Pope Leo XIV on Monday after criticizing the pontiff for his opposition to the war in Iran — and he sought to explain away a now-deleted social media post depicting himself as Jesus by saying he had thought the image was of him as a doctor. Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has called Trump's remarks "unacceptable". FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta reports from Rome.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump says he won't apologize to Pope Leo after criticism

events.title: Leo becomes first Pope to visit Algeria at start of major Africa tour
events.description: Leo XIV wants the world's attention on a continent vital to the Church's future, an aide says.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Brazil’s former spy chief who fled country arrested by ICE agents in US
events.description: Alexandre Ramagem fled country after he was sentenced to 16 years for his role in plotting military coup in Brazil When Brazil ’s former president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to nearly 30 years in prison for an attempted coup, six other members of his cabinet were also found guilty and all began serving their sentences – except for one. Days before the verdict, Alexandre Ramagem, Bolsonaro’s former spy chief, fled by car to Guyana and boarded a flight to the United States, where he has remained ever since. Continue reading...

events.title: Bosses of Santos, Woodside, Chevron and Shell asked to give evidence to Greens-led gas tax inquiry
events.description: Labor is under pressure to impose a new 25% export tax amid soaring prices from the global fuel shock Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The bosses of resources giants including Santos, Woodside, Chevron and Shell could be compelled to face an inquiry into export tax settings, as the Greens ramp up pressure on Labor before the budget. The chief executives of the companies, along with the bosses of gas exporters Inpex and ConocoPhillips, have been requested to give evidence to a Greens-led inquiry sitting in Canberra and Perth later this month. Under Senate rules, they could be compelled to attend if they choose not to give evidence voluntarily. Continue reading...

events.title: End of the Orban era: Hungary and Europe breathe again

events.title: Albanese didn’t return with shiploads of diesel. That doesn’t mean his Singapore visit wasn’t a success
events.description: Having received assurances from Singapore over refined fuels, diesel supply will surely be next on the prime minister’s agenda Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese isn’t coming back from Singapore with a shipload of diesel in his checked baggage. That doesn’t mean his whistle-stop visit wasn’t a success, or that it won’t be seen in future as a pivotal moment if fuel stocks continue to be choked by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The government never expected that the quick whip to Singapore, with just one full day on the ground, would elicit a new supply of petrol or diesel. Singapore already supplies 55% of Australia’s unleaded , 22% of jet fuel and 15% of diesel. Continue reading...

events.title: Interfaith dialogue: 'The messenger of peace in the name of Pope Leo XIV is coming to visit us'

From his experience of living over a decade in Algeria, he challenges inherited narratives shaped by the violence of the 1990s, highlighting the present reality of coexistence and tolerance. He argues that interreligious dialogue must extend beyond institutions into families and daily life.

The 11-day trip, which will include stops in Cameroon, Angola and Equatorial Guinea, is the longest by Pope Leo since being elected to the papacy in May last year. Continue reading...

events.title: And the election winner is … the candidate who can afford Africa’s soaring nomination fees
events.description: Presidential elections in Djibouti and Benin at the weekend highlighted how a costly electoral system is reshaping democracy Alexis Mohamed would have loved to stand against his former boss. A longtime adviser to Djibouti’s president, Ismail Omar Guelleh, Mohamed resigned last September, citing democratic regression in the country. But at the election at the weekend, Mohamed was not on the ballot. Now outside the country, he says he cannot return home to file nomination papers or campaign freely without credible security guarantees. Even if he were allowed to compete, nomination costs would still loom as a steep barrier in a political environment many critics describe as ceremonial, with Guelleh the habitual winner. Continue reading...

events.title: Bahamas police again question US man over disappearance of wife at sea
events.description: Brian Hooker says wife Lynette fell overboard from dinghy but family members have cast doubt on that account Police in the Bahamas on Monday were set to again interview a US man who said his wife fell overboard from their boat. In a statement on Sunday to the Guardian, Brian Hooker’s attorney, Terrel Butler, said: “The police have requested another interview with [Brian Hooker] tomorrow.” Continue reading...

events.title: Jorginho calls Chappell Roan security incident a ‘misunderstanding’
events.description: Flamengo footballer previously accused pop star’s security of aggressive behavior to his 11-year-old stepdaughter The Chappell Roan security incident raises a bigger question: what do celebrities owe their fans? The Flamengo footballer Jorginho has clarified his comments on last month’s incident between his 11-year-old stepdaughter and a security guard in Brazil, calling his previous claims against Chappell Roan “a misunderstanding”. “I made my initial statement in the heat of the moment, after hearing that my child and wife had been approached by an adult male security guard in an intimidating way,” Jorginho wrote on Instagram. “I reacted as any father would. My priority is, and always will be, protecting my family, and that is exactly what I did.” Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Jorginho calls Chappell Roan security incident a ‘misunderstanding’

events.title: Peru extends voting for president into Monday after chaos at polling stations

In an unprecedented move, Peru’s electoral agency ONPE announced on Sunday night that it would extend voting for an extra day to allow tens of thousands of Peruvians in the country and abroad, who had been unable to vote, to cast their ballots. Continue reading...

As the opposition loses support to Pauline Hanson’s One Nation, Taylor says too many people seek to use Australia’s generosity “for self-serving purposes”, promising to speed up rejections of asylum seekers from countries deemed safe to return to. events.title: Trump vows to sink Iranian ships approaching a U.S. blockade of Strait of Hormuz
events.description: President Trump said the U.S. would interdict vessels that had paid what he called an "illegal toll" to Iran to cross the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Trump says U.S. will blockade Iranian ports after peace talks fail
events.description: On Sunday, President Trump said the U.S. would blockade the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations between the U.S. and Iran broke down over the weekend.

events.title: Morning news brief
events.description: President Trump says U.S. will blockade the Strait of Hormuz, failed U.S.-Iran negotiations cause oil prices to surge, Hungary's Viktor Orbán loses reelection bid.

events.title: Oil prices surge after U.S.-Iran peace talks break down
events.description: The U.S. said on Sunday that it will soon block ships from Iranian ports. It comes after the U.S. and Iran failed to reach an agreement during face-to-face peace talks, sending oil prices surging.

events.title: US judge dismisses Trump's lawsuit against Wall Street Journal
events.description: The judge threw out Donald Trump's lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal over a story on the US president's ties to late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

events.title: EU applauds Hungary’s election result
events.description: After Hungary’s opposition ousted Viktor Orban after 16 years in power, hopes rise in Brussels that Budapest may abandon its obstructionist course. What is the EU expecting from Peter Magyar?

events.title: Trump posts and then deletes Jesus-like AI image of himself
events.description: The US president denied looking like Jesus in the image, saying he thought it was himself "as a doctor." Trump did not explain why he deleted the post.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Trump posts and then deletes Jesus-like AI image of himself

events.title: Dieselgate: Audi bosses, engineers set for long trial as plea deal fails
events.description: Two former Audi bosses and two engineers are facing charges over "Dieselgate," a massive emissions scandal that still haunts Germany's automobile sector. As no plea deals could be reached, a long trial seems likely.

events.title: Germany: 1 in 4 residents has 'immigration history'
events.description: Every fourth person living in Germany has either moved there from abroad or been born to parents who are both immigrants. The new statistics are a modest increase on the 2024 figures.

events.title: Peru general elections: Fujimori leading, runoff likely
events.description: Keiko Fujimori looked set to face a conservative rival in a runoff vote. More than 30 candidates had been contention for the presidency.

events.title: Orban ousted: What Magyar’s victory means for Hungary and the EU
events.description: Peter Magyar's landslide victory over Viktor Orban in Sunday’s Hungarian legislative elections marks a seismic shift for Hungary and the EU, but Brussels may need to temper its enthusiasm. On the rule of law, migration and LGBTQ rights, the road ahead is neither straight nor guaranteed.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Orban ousted: What Magyar’s victory means for Hungary and the EU

events.title: Benin's opposition candidate concedes defeat to Wadagni in presidential vote
events.description: Benin opposition candidate Paul Hounkpe suffered a bruising election loss on Monday after conceding to favourite Romuald Wadagni in Benin's presidential vote.

events.title: Trump has 'destroyed his credibility' by posting AI image of himself as Jesus, says former Italian PM
events.description: Donald Trump has deleted an AI image of himself as Jesus Christ as he stood by his criticism of Pope Leo.

events.title: Rory McIlroy wanted to 'prove last year wasn't a fluke' with historic second Masters win
events.description: Rory McIlroy has achieved a historic win at the US Masters golf tournament, taking the title for the second year in a row.

events.title: Man arrested over alleged damage to US military plane at airport
events.description: A man has been arrested over alleged criminal damage of a US military plane at an Irish airport.

events.title: China unveils measures to improve ties with Taiwan after visit by opposition leader
events.description: China has said it is willing to resume some ties it had suspended with Taiwan, such as direct flights to cities across China, allowing in "healthy" television dramas and facilitating food sales.

events.title: Legendary Bollywood singer Asha Bhosle dies aged 92
events.description: Indian singer Asha Bhosle, who sang on tracks for countless Bollywood films, has died at the age of 92.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Legendary Bollywood singer Asha Bhosle dies aged 92

events.title: Philippine President Marcos debunks health rumors with jumping jacks
events.description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has dismissed rumors about his health by doing jumping jacks and jogging outside his office

events.title: Magyar wants to take over as prime minister as early as May 5
events.description: Péter Magyar has called on Hungary’s president to convene parliament to form a new government quickly

events.title: Hungary's election winner Magyar seeks to be sworn in as prime minister as early as May 5
events.description: Hungary's election winner Magyar seeks to be sworn in as prime minister as early as May 5

events.title: European Union support for the UN ‘more vital than ever’, Security Council hears
events.description: A senior United Nations official on Monday hailed the European Union (EU) as “a major economic and diplomatic actor and a strong advocate of multilateralism.”

events.title: New era for space dawns as Artemis astronauts return
events.description: After a 10-day journey around the far side of the Moon, the four astronauts on NASA’s Artemis II mission are set to splash down in the Pacific Ocean late on Friday.

events.title: Eric Swalwell faces Manhattan sex assault probe after ending California governor campaign amid allegations
events.description: Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg's office is investigating sex assault allegations against Rep. Eric Swalwell, who suspended his California governor campaign.

events.title: Florida teacher accused of sex with student whose parents tracked his location
events.description: Florida math teacher Kirsten Rose faces five counts of unlawful sexual activity with a minor after authorities say the student's parents tracked their son's location.

events.title: WATCH: Artemis II astronaut Christina Koch reunites with dog after 10 days in space
events.description: Christina Koch reunites with her dog in emotional video after returning from Artemis II, NASA's historic 10-day moon mission around the lunar far side.

events.title: FBI raids Texas home of suspect accused of throwing Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman's San Francisco house
events.description: FBI agents raided the Spring, Texas, home of a 20-year-old suspect accused of hurling a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's San Francisco house.

events.title: Eric Swalwell exits California governor race amid graphic accusations of misconduct and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Starving toddler ate diapers and drywall before dying in filthy room while parents lived clean, police say
events.description: An Indiana couple was arrested and hit with neglect and murder charges after their starving toddler died from eating diapers and drywall to survive.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Starving toddler ate diapers and drywall before dying in filthy room while parents lived clean, police say

events.title: Iran secures UN role with backing from UK, France, Canada, Australia as US stands alone
events.description: Western democracies face backlash for allowing Iran and other authoritarian regimes to secure influential U.N. seats as the U.S. stood alone in opposition.

events.title: Could Somaliland base emerge as US foothold against Iran, Houthis in key sea lanes?
events.description: Somaliland is offering the U.S. a Red Sea port and air base at Berbera as Iran pushes Houthis to disrupt shipping through the Bab-el-Mandeb strait.

events.title: China says it will resume some ties with Taiwan after visit by opposition leader
events.description: China says it will restore some ties with Taiwan, including direct flights and aquaculture imports, following a visit by opposition leader Cheng Li-wun.

events.title: Pope Leo XIV to visit fastest-growing Catholic continent during 4-nation Africa trip
events.description: Pope Leo XIV begins a four-nation African visit Monday, traveling to the fastest-growing continent for the Catholic Church on his fourth foreign trip.

events.title: Malnourished boy found locked in father's van for more than a year unable to walk, hadn't showered since 2024
events.description: A malnourished 9-year-old boy allegedly locked in his father's van in eastern France was found in the fetal position surrounded by trash and excrement.

events.title: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi in Beijing as Taiwan defense fight intensifies
events.description: Xi Jinping met KMT leader Cheng Li-wun in Beijing as Taiwan's opposition blocks a $40 billion defense budget amid U.S. pressure to approve it.

events.title: Israeli man built bomb lab for Iranian plot targeting ex-PM Bennett, authorities say
events.description: A Haifa man was allegedly recruited by an Iranian handler to build bombs for an assassination plot believed to target ex-PM Naftali Bennett.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israeli man built bomb lab for Iranian plot targeting ex-PM Bennett, authorities say
